steps.title: Set the numerator to zero
steps.content:
A fraction equals $0$ when its numerator equals $0$ and its denominator does not equal $0$.
$$(x-9)(x-10)=0$$

steps.title: Find the valid solutions
steps.content: The numerator is $0$ at $x=9$ and $x=10$. Neither value makes the denominator $x-4$ equal to $0$, so both are valid.

steps.title: Add the solutions
steps.content: Add the two valid solutions. $$9+10=19$$ The answer is C.
